info.name: PAY-3821 Treasury funding-batch send error handling
info.description:
HTTP companion for the PAY-3821 E2E/QA test plan (e2e-test-plan.md, same folder).

WHAT THIS COVERS (HTTP-driveable slices only):
- Health check (GET /health) -> 200
- Trigger the funding-batch send (POST /send-funding-batch) -> 202 + captures correlationId
- Re-trigger after config restore (recovery / idempotent retry)
- OPTIONAL: App Insights data-plane query by correlationId (needs separate AAD token + App ID)

WHAT THIS DOES NOT COVER (do manually per the plan):
- Staging FundingBatches rows (SQL)
- Inducing the SFTP failure (env config change)
- Asserting RequestSentTimeStamp (SQL)
- Asserting ACTUAL email delivery (SendGrid Activity + inbox/M365 quarantine)

STAGE SETUP (verified): `baseUrl` = https://stage-platform.wellfit.com/treasury and header `api_key` (not Bearer). Same as the Treasury API collection. Postman environment = **No environment**.

The send is async on a background coordinator: 202 means ENQUEUED, not sent. Use the captured
correlationId to find the async outcome in App Insights after a short settle delay.

variable.value: <App Insights API key OR use AAD bearer>

exec: pm.test('TC-01 step1 / TC-02 step8: health is 200 OK', function () {

request.description: Public, no auth. Confirms the service is alive — used as the baseline (TC-01) and as the post-failure liveness check (TC-02/03 step 8: no host-loop death).

exec: // Endpoint enqueues a Send work item: 202 on success, 503 if the coordinator queue is full.
pm.test('202 body has correlationId + stage=Send', function () {
console.log('Captured correlationId = ' + body.correlationId + '  -> use this to query App Insights');
console.log('503 queue full — retry shortly (runbook retry-storm protection).');
// REMINDER: 202 != send succeeded. The SFTP send + PAY-3821 failure handling run async on
// IFundingBatchCoordinator. After a settle delay (~connect timeout, 20s+), verify the real
// outcome via App Insights (request 4), DB (RequestSentTimeStamp), and email delivery (SendGrid

request.description: STAGE: use header `api_key` (Treasury API collection pattern), not Bearer. Returns 202 + {correlationId, stage:'Send'} (enqueued) or 503 (queue full). Stage SQL rows + failure trigger BEFORE calling this for AC tests.

exec: // After this, verify in DB that the previously-stranded rows now have RequestSentTimeStamp SET
// (idempotent retry intact). Restore config to good values BEFORE running this request.

request.description: Identical to request 2; separated for the TC-04 recovery leg. Run AFTER restoring config — confirms stranded batches send + stamp normally.

exec: // OPTIONAL / ADVANCED. Requires App Insights Application ID + an API key (or swap to AAD bearer).
// Adjust the table (traces/exceptions) and timespan to your env. This is a convenience only —
// the plan's primary evidence is the App Insights portal view filtered by correlationId.
pm.test('a failure record references the correlationId', function () {

url.raw: https://api.applicationinsights.io/v1/apps/{{appInsightsAppId}}/query?timespan=PT30M&query=union traces, exceptions | where customDimensions.CorrelationId == '{{correlationId}}' or operation_Id == '{{correlationId}}' | where message has 'Funding batch send' or type contains 'Exception' | project timestamp, message, severityLevel, customDimensions | order by timestamp desc

query.value: union traces, exceptions | where customDimensions.CorrelationId == '{{correlationId}}' or operation_Id == '{{correlationId}}' | where message has 'Funding batch send' or type contains 'Exception' | project timestamp, message, severityLevel, customDimensions | order by timestamp desc

request.description: OPTIONAL. App Insights data-plane REST query (KQL) to pull the async failure log by the captured correlationId. Needs the Application ID + an API key, OR replace x-api-key with an AAD bearer token (Authorization: Bearer <aad-token> for api://api.applicationinsights.io). Verify the column name carrying the correlation id (operation_Id vs a custom dimension) in your env and adjust the KQL.
